rationale: The cited paper is used in the mechanism-building background, not just listed in a generic citation cluster. The citing authors explicitly attribute to Dellaert and Haubl (2012) the finding that recommendations shift consumers from continuing search to evaluating already-inspected alternatives. They then use this logic to motivate their prediction and interpretation that targeted promotions reduce exploration and can crowd out cross-genre purchases. Even though it is one of several supporting studies, it plays a meaningful conceptual role in framing the paper's search mechanism.
relation_description: The citing paper relies on Dellaert and Haubl (2012) as prior evidence that recommendation structures alter search behavior toward within-set evaluation. It uses that prior result as conceptual support for its own argument that targeted promotions narrow search and generate cross-genre crowding-out effects.
